skill.name: specdd-orient
skill.description: Read-only orientation skill for SpecDD projects. Loads the bootstrap chain and project specs to prepare the agent for later work, without making any edits.
skill.summary: Read-only orientation for SpecDD projects that prepares the agent for later work without making any edits.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: The skill is a benign read-only orientation document for SpecDD projects. All 8 static findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives: the 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flag at line 3 is a misidentification of a description field, and the 7 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' flags are markdown code formatting of file paths and skill names, not actual command execution. No code execution, network access, file modification, or prompt injection was found. The skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged backtick-delimited file paths and skill names (e.g., `.specdd/bootstrap.md`, `specdd-cli`) as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are standard markdown inline code formatting, not command execution. No shell invocation or code evaluation occurs in this file.

low_findings.title: Description field misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged line 3 as a 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. Line 3 contains the skill's description metadata field, which has no cryptographic content. This is a false positive from pattern matching on benign YAML text.

content.user_title: Orient Yourself in Any SpecDD Project
content.value_statement: Starting work in a SpecDD project without understanding the bootstrap chain leads to wrong assumptions about authority. This skill loads the right context so you can begin later tasks with confidence.
actual_capabilities: Reads the .specdd/bootstrap.md file to confirm a project follows SpecDD conventions
Loads project and local bootstrap overrides in the correct precedence order
Identifies the root or main project spec named by the bootstrap chain
Inspects top-level specs needed to understand repository authority
Checks current git status to distinguish existing work from new changes
limitations: Makes no file edits and performs no deep analysis unless explicitly asked
Does not infer write authority for future tasks beyond the active bootstrap
Cannot bootstrap a non-SpecDD project; use specdd-adopt for that purpose
Orientation context is not a substitute for full spec-chain resolution on later tasks

use_cases.title: Prepare before a SpecDD implementation task
use_cases.description: Load the bootstrap chain and main project spec before starting a coding task so you understand the governing authority.
use_cases.target_user: Developers starting implementation work in a SpecDD repo

use_cases.title: Audit project authority for a code review
use_cases.description: Identify which specs control the area under review and check git status before commenting on changes.
use_cases.target_user: Reviewers and tech leads evaluating pull requests

use_cases.title: Onboard a new agent to a SpecDD repository
use_cases.description: Quickly orient a new AI agent session to the project structure and governing specs without making any edits.
use_cases.target_user: Teams handing off work between AI agent sessions

prompt_templates.title: Basic orientation request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the specdd-orient skill to get oriented in this project. Report the bootstrap files read and the main project authority.
prompt_templates.scenario: First interaction with a new SpecDD project

prompt_templates.title: Orientation before a planned edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run specdd-orient to understand the project. After orientation, summarize which specs govern the area I want to edit.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing for a targeted code change

prompt_templates.title: Orientation with git status check
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-orient and include the current git status in your report so I can see pending changes.

prompt_templates.title: Deep orientation with CLI assistance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-orient combined with the specdd-cli skill for CLI-assisted orientation. Treat CLI output as context only.
prompt_templates.scenario: Large or complex SpecDD projects needing extra tooling

output_examples.input: Use specdd-orient to get oriented in this project.
output: Orientation complete. Ready for follow-up tasks.

output_examples.input: Run specdd-orient and include git status.
output: Git status: 2 modified files, 1 untracked file
No immediate blockers for follow-up tasks.

best_practices: Always read the bootstrap chain in the documented order to get the correct precedence
Reread bootstrap files only when the project root changes or a file may have been updated
Check git status during orientation to separate existing work from new changes
anti_patterns: Do not edit files during orientation; this skill is read-only by design
Do not treat orientation context as write authority for later tasks
Do not skip to implementation before orientation is complete

faq.question: Does specdd-orient modify any files?
faq.answer: No. This skill is strictly read-only and will not edit, create, or delete files in the project.

faq.question: What if .specdd/bootstrap.md is missing?
faq.answer: The skill will not proceed. Use the specdd-adopt skill only if the operator explicitly asks to adopt SpecDD.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to get full spec-chain resolution?
faq.answer: No. Orientation is lighter than full spec-chain resolution. For concrete tasks, rerun the normal SpecDD spec-chain resolution.

faq.question: When should I reread the bootstrap files?
faq.answer: Reread them only when the active project root changes, a bootstrap file may have changed, or you need exact text for a decision or quote.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to run automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. It only reads files and reports findings. It does not execute commands, access the network, or modify the filesystem.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: It is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
